message:
security: truncate API error response bodies to 2KB across 20 connectors

Add body.truncate(2048) after resp.text().await in error-handling paths
across all connector clients that read the full response body on HTTP
errors. This prevents unbounded memory allocation when a malicious or
misconfigured upstream API returns a multi-megabyte error response (e.g.,
an HTML error page, a debug dump, or a deliberately oversized payload).

The truncation is applied after the body is read (so the full response
is consumed from the connection) but before it's stored in error structs,
logged, or parsed as JSON. 2KB is sufficient for any structured API
error message while capping memory usage.

Affected connectors (20):
box, circleci, docusign, elasticsearch, evernote, figma (4 error paths),
gitlab, google-docs, homeassistant, hubspot, intercom, logseq, monday,
postgresql, redis, salesforce, sendgrid, spotify, terraform, zapier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
